nodes.name: Executable Specification
bullets: Keep harness control flow in a repo-owned artifact instead of burying it in controller code.
Branches, loops, parallelism, retries, and handoffs should be inspectable and diffable as product logic.
For PlotLot, the zoning/site-feasibility workflow should be editable as a stage graph: intake -> retrieve -> extract -> verify -> calculate -> report -> review.

nodes.name: Step-Bounded Context
bullets: Context should be granted per step, not inherited by default from one ever-growing transcript.
Fresh-task vs persistent-step semantics let the harness decide when to isolate context and when to preserve it.
For PlotLot, ordinance extraction should see only the parcel facts and cited ordinance sections needed for that extraction lane.

nodes.name: Workflow Module Interface
bullets: Treat skills, tools, and subagents as workflow modules with named parameters and typed return values.
A reusable module boundary is the unit of delegation, testing, and replacement.
PlotLot modules should include parcel facts, zoning retrieval, dimensional extraction, conflict resolution, deterministic calculation, and evidence review.

nodes.name: Replayable Trajectory
bullets: Checkpoint after each stage with step id, context snapshot, metrics, tool outputs, and sandbox/workspace state.
Selective replay lets you hold upstream evidence constant while changing a downstream prompt or verifier.
For PlotLot, replay should avoid redoing GIS and ordinance fetches when only extraction, report, or review logic changes.

nodes.name: Workflow Verification
bullets: Define preconditions and postconditions at the workflow-step level so runs are testable as trajectories, not just final answers.
Use both static checks over the plan and dynamic checks over the realized execution trace.
For PlotLot, verification means every extracted zoning number is unit-normalized, source-cited, and reproducible by deterministic calculators.

nodes.name: Agent-Supervised Tool Adaptation
bullets: Freeze the backbone and improve the surrounding searchers, memory writers, planners, and reviewers under its supervision.
Narrow procedural specialists are often more data-efficient and less forgetful than retraining one monolithic agent.
For PlotLot, ordinance retrieval, extraction, evidence review, and memory curation should evolve as peripheral modules around a stable orchestrator.

nodes.name: Adaptation Signal Design
bullets: Separate dense causal execution signals from sparse holistic utility signals; they answer different questions.
Use execution-grounded metrics to debug tool mechanics and end-to-end metrics to judge final analyst value.
For PlotLot, citation resolution, parser correctness, and calculator reproducibility are execution signals; report usefulness and analyst acceptance are holistic signals.

nodes.name: Graduated Subagent
bullets: A specialist can be learned under A1/A2-style adaptation, then frozen and redeployed as a reusable T1 tool.
Graduation turns fragile prompt behavior into a stable interface with measurable responsibilities.
For PlotLot, stable ordinance search, extraction, or review specialists should be promoted into reusable modules callable by the main harness.

nodes.name: Explicit-to-Implicit Translation
bullets: Convert directly observed artifacts into model-legible signals before the agent starts searching or planning.
Keep static translation tools outside the active agent loop; they reduce ambiguity without spending tool budget.
For PlotLot, translate parcel/zoning pages, ordinance PDFs, tables, and map legends into normalized jurisdiction, code, and evidence candidates before LLM synthesis.

nodes.name: Dynamic Reference Expansion
bullets: When a static registry is incomplete, the harness should actively discover additional references during execution.
Reference expansion should be budgeted, queryable, and evidence-producing rather than free-form browsing.
For PlotLot, discover official parcel, zoning, and ordinance hosts from jurisdiction clues instead of assuming complete prior coverage.

nodes.name: Entity-to-Authority Verification
bullets: Predict the governing entity, then verify that the source boundary is official before trusting downstream claims.
Authority verification should absorb domain variants and mirrors while still distinguishing official hosts from lookalikes.
For PlotLot, after predicting city/county/ordinance authority, verify official municipal, county, ArcGIS, or Municode domains before accepting zoning facts.

nodes.name: Budgeted Tool Loop
bullets: Dynamic discovery improves accuracy, but the harness must explicitly limit retrieval rounds, latency, and spend.
Tool loops should expose cost/benefit tradeoffs and stop conditions rather than silently accumulating work.
For PlotLot, ordinance retrieval and evidence resolution should run under explicit budgets for tool calls, tokens, and wall-clock time.

overviewConnections.title: AgentSPEX (2604.13346v1) synthesis
bullets: Connects [[Harness Runtime]] to [[Executable Specification]]: the harness becomes an interpreter/executor for a repo-owned workflow artifact.
Connects [[Context Broker]] to [[Step-Bounded Context]]: task-vs-step semantics make context scope an explicit engineering choice.
Connects [[Evidence Ledger]] to [[Workflow Verification]] and [[Replayable Trajectory]]: the same saved artifacts that support replay also support post-hoc audit and deterministic recomputation.
For PlotLot, this paper sharpens the target architecture for a land-use/site-feasibility harness: modular workflow stages, explicit evidence gates, and replayable vertical runs.

overviewConnections.title: Adaptation of Agentic AI (2512.16301v3) synthesis
bullets: Connects [[Harness Runtime]] to [[Agent-Supervised Tool Adaptation]]: the stable orchestrator should supervise evolving specialist modules rather than absorb every improvement into one agent policy.
Connects [[Evidence Ledger]] to [[Adaptation Signal Design]]: evidence packets and deterministic calculators let PlotLot manufacture A1-style execution signals inside a broader T2 workflow.
Connects [[Workflow Module Interface]] to [[Graduated Subagent]]: once a specialist proves reliable, it should be frozen behind a typed interface and reused across runs.
For PlotLot, this paper supports a frozen-core / adaptive-periphery architecture for the land-use/site-feasibility harness.

overviewConnections.title: Automated Phishing Detection using URLs and Webpages (2408.01667v2) synthesis
bullets: Connects [[Context Broker]] to [[Explicit-to-Implicit Translation]]: before context selection, the harness should translate raw artifacts into higher-order signals.
Connects [[Harness Runtime]] to [[Dynamic Reference Expansion]] and [[Budgeted Tool Loop]]: dynamic discovery beats static coverage, but only if retrieval budgets are explicit.
Connects [[Governance Middleware]] and [[Evidence Ledger]] to [[Entity-to-Authority Verification]]: first verify the official source boundary, then accept downstream claims.

For PlotLot, the direct analogue is jurisdiction/source discovery: predict the governing city/county plus ordinance host, verify official domains, then cache trusted references for future site-feasibility runs.
AgentSPEX is the clearest reviewed paper so far on converting harness logic into an executable, human-editable workflow spec.
Its strongest contribution for PlotLot is not the YAML syntax itself, but the combination of explicit context boundaries, checkpointed execution, and replayable downstream experimentation.
Its main gap relative to other reviewed governance papers is weak policy language; PlotLot should pair this workflow style with stronger approval and least-privilege controls.
This is the clearest reviewed paper so far on where adaptation should live in an agentic system.
For PlotLot, its most important consequence is strategic: freeze the orchestrator, and improve the surrounding retrieval, memory, review, and evidence modules.
Use dense execution signals wherever possible inside the land-use harness, and reserve holistic judging for truly end-to-end synthesis quality.
The paper matters less for phishing per se than for its harness decomposition: static signal translation -> budgeted discovery -> authority verification.
Its most transferable primitive for PlotLot is replacing exhaustive prior registries with on-run reference expansion backed by official-source verification.

Its main warning is operational: dynamic discovery improved accuracy dramatically but paid ~20s/sample and depended on brittle external services, so PlotLot needs caching, fallback routes, and strict retrieval budgets.
